pterodactyl-tall.motifs: Sky motif differs from the sibling: this page shipped a WARM GOLD SUN in the 3.1 wave while pterodactyl-wide has a CRESCENT MOON (gemini-3.1-migration.md). Nothing enforces motif consistency (ISSUES) — a regen of either page should decide deliberately whether to match.

pterodactyl-wide.motifs: Sky motif differs from the sibling: this page has a CRESCENT MOON while pterodactyl-tall shipped a WARM GOLD SUN in the 3.1 wave (gemini-3.1-migration.md). Nothing enforces motif consistency (ISSUES) — a regen of either page should decide deliberately whether to match.

retry.notes: the eye's pupil opening must be SOLID NEAR-BLACK, almost pure black, much darker than the dinosaur's green body and the night sky, with NO white catchlight painted inside the eye (the line art already provides the eye's white); keep the whole head and muzzle the same green as the body; paint the back plates, round back spots, and tail spikes plain flat warm amber-gold with no vein lines or patterns

night.review: Judge the eye on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE, not the raw fill: this chalk whitens most of the eye (a solid sclera/shine teardrop that merges with the eye ring at the bottom) and leaves only a small pupil opening — exactly where fill takes like to paint a white catchlight. Expect the composited pupil to read as a solid dark crescent wrapping the chalk's white patch (a full dark disc is unreachable with this chalk); ANY white the fill paints inside the eye composites into a blank white orb. audit-fill-eyes passes both the defective and the fixed takes — only composite review judges this eye.
night.why: 2026-07-13 ISSUES Tier 1 #1 fix (this session): the previous seeded 'with only a small white catchlight' note made things worse — the model kept painting that catchlight in the chalk's only fillable pupil opening. The no-catchlight phrasing above, plus pinning the body/plate colors against re-rolls (teal plates, tan muzzle, plate veins appeared on unpinned takes), shipped on take 8 of 8 at default temperature.

retry.notes: give the eye a solid dark pupil clearly darker than the night sky, with only a small white catchlight

night.review: Judge the eye on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE, not the raw fill: expect a solid dark-brown pupil around the white catchlight inside the white sclera ring. The pre-fix 2026-07 take painted the pupil the same navy as the night sky, which composited into a blank white orb while every gate passed — gates alone prove nothing here.
night.why: 2026-07-13 ISSUES Tier 1 #1 fix (this session): regenerated with the retry note above, first take shipped. audit-fill-eyes reported night ok on the defective take too, so keep judging this eye on the composite.
